articles.title: UK Natural Gas Futures Fall from 3-Year High
articles.expand: UK natural gas futures fell below 125 pence per therm, retreating from a three-year high of 146.3 reached on March 9, after President Donald Trump signalled that the conflict with Iran could soon come to an end. Sentiment was further supported by a statement from G7 finance ministers, who said they stand ready to release strategic oil reserves if n...

articles.title: Bund Yields Ease as Hopes Grow for End to Iran Conflict
articles.expand: Germany’s 10-year Bund yield, the benchmark for European borrowing costs, eased to 2.84% after nearing 2.9% on Monday, amid hopes for a swift end to the war in Iran. Markets found some temporary relief after US President Trump indicated that the military operation in Iran could conclude soon and is progressing well ahead of the initially projected ...

articles.title: Sterling Strengthens from 3-Month Low
articles.expand: The British pound rose to $1.346, rebounding from a three-month low of $1.335 reached on March 3, as investors moved out of the US dollar and back into other major currencies on hopes that the conflict will prove less inflationary than previously feared. Market sentiment improved as oil prices retreated, helped by efforts from US President Donald T...

articles.title: BCE: Precaución con las apuestas de un ajuste restrictivo más suave – BNY
articles.expand: Bob Savage, Jefe de Estrategia Macro de Mercados de BNY, señala que los mercados monetarios reducen las expectativas de endurecimiento del Banco Central Europeo.

articles.title: ECB: Caution with softer tightening bets – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage notes that money markets scale back expectations for European Central Bank tightening.

articles.title: Emergency measures by US and Saudis calm markets (USD/JPY may fall and GBP/USD may rise)
articles.expand: In the face of ongoing hostilities, oil has steadied after a near 30% jump on Monday to about $120 per barrel. The United States began emergency measures to curb the rise in oil futures, and Saudi Arabia reportedly increased pipeline flows, from the previously reported 2 million barrels per day to as much as 5 million. Those steps have weighed on p...

articles.title: Oro: Las expectativas de la Fed respaldan el precio a medio plazo – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Thu Lan Nguyen de Commerzbank informa que el Oro se ha recuperado ya que la señal de Trump de un posible fin de la guerra en Irán ha aliviado los temores de una subida de tasas.

articles.title: Gold: Fed expectations support medium-term prices – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Thu Lan Nguyen reports that Gold has recovered as Trump’s signal of a possible end to the Iran war eased rate-hike fears.

articles.title: British Analysis 10/03: Pound Price – GBP Finds Bounce After
articles.expand: The British pound initially fell a bit against the US dollar as the markets tried to price in a massive escalation of the war in Iran.

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Slow creep higher risk – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes that the Pound has outperformed since late February, while the Euro has lagged, with recent EUR/GBP gains largely tied to fading expectations of a March BoE rate cut and positioning effects.

Sentiment improved later in the day as oil prices fell sharply following the G7’s indication that it may release emergency oil reserves. articles.expand: US Dollar Index trades near 99 ahead of CPI data as Middle East tensions lift safe-haven demand. Can GBP/USD and EUR/USD sustain recovery?

articles.title: Reajuste del BoE y zona de soporte del EUR/GBP – ING
articles.expand: Chris Turner, analista de ING, destaca que los mercados de tasas del Reino Unido han experimentado uno de los mayores reajustes debido al shock energético, reflejando la alta inflación en el Reino Unido y un Banco de Inglaterra relativamente restrictivo.

articles.title: GBP: BoE repricing and EUR/GBP support zone – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chris Turner highlights that UK rate markets have seen one of the largest repricings from the energy shock, reflecting high UK inflation and a relatively hawkish Bank of England.

articles.expand: Since the clashes began on February 28 between US and Israeli forces and Iran, the energy sector has suffered a sharp shock: an estimated one-fifth of global oil and natural gas supplies have been taken offline, triggering a surge in prices and renewed calls to accelerate the switch to domestic renewable energy. articles.title: RBA: El mercado evalúa el riesgo de una subida de tipos de interés en marzo – TD Securities
articles.expand: El estratega senior de TD Securities, Prashant Newnaha, destaca que los recientes comentarios del vicegobernador del RBA, Andrew Hauser, suenan a línea dura y refuerzan el mensaje de la gobernadora Bullock de que la reunión del Consejo de marzo está activa.

articles.title: RBA: Market weighs live March hike risk – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Senior Strategist Prashant Newnaha highlights that recent comments from RBA Deputy Governor Hauser sound hawkish and reinforce Governor Bullock’s message that the March Board meeting is live.

articles.title: JPY: Las perspectivas de subida de tipos de interés en junio respaldan al Yen – ING
articles.expand: El Economista Senior de ING, Min Joo Kang, argumenta que los datos más fuertes del PIB de Japón y el gasto privado resiliente apoyan la continuación de la normalización de la política del Banco de Japón (BoJ), siendo más probable una subida de tasas en junio que en abril.

articles.title: JPY: June hike prospects support currency – ING
articles.expand: ING's Senior Economist Min Joo Kang argues that stronger Japan GDP data and resilient private spending support continued Bank of Japan (BoJ) policy normalisation, with a rate hike more likely in June than April.

articles.expand: If the CPI data meets expectations or comes in a bit softer, NZD/USD and USD/CHF’s charts could start leaning toward dollar weakness.

articles.title: Australia NAB business confidence turns negative after RBA hike
articles.expand: Australia’s NAB Business confidence fell sharply by five points to -1 February, slipping into negative territory for the first time in eleven months. In contrast, business conditions held steady at +7, roughly in line with the long-run average. The split suggests that firms are still experiencing stable trading conditions but are becoming increasin...

articles.expand: Australia’s Westpac Consumer Sentiment index edged up 1.2% mom to 91.6 in March. While sentiment remains firmly in pessimistic territory, the survey indicates that consumers have responded less negatively than expected to the RBA’s 25 bps increase in February. articles.title: Loonie backs off a month high against the greenback
articles.expand: The Canadian dollar slipped on Monday from a level close to its highest point in nearly a month against the US dollar, but it continued to post gains against some other G10 currencies as the surge in oil prices driven by the war in the Middle East influenced investor sentiment.

articles.title: Swiss Franc Rises As Iran Crisis Deepens
articles.expand: The Swiss franc strengthened against other major currencies in the European session on Monday, as European stocks fell sharply to hit a two-month low as investors braced for a prolonged conflict in the Middle East that could send energy costs even higher, risking inflation and regional economic growth.

articles.title: Dollar Rises on Haven Bid, Oil as Iran War Rips Across Markets
articles.expand: The dollar strengthened against every major currency Monday as a deepening war in the Middle East pushed oil prices above $100 a barrel and boosted demand for havens.
